content:
An example might be: The dress was beautiful but it was too expensive so I didn't buy it.

This sentence can be corrected by adding commas.

The correct form is: The dress was beautiful, but it was too expensive, so I didn't buy it.

To avoid run-on sentences, ensure each independent clause is punctuated adequately with a comma, conjunction, or semicolon. If you're unsure whether your sentence is a run-on, try reading it aloud and see if you naturally pause at any point. If so, that's where you should insert your punctuation mark.
